steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: The passage places Drelichman and Voth's research alongside Brewer's earlier work on European early modern state finance, and notes their unique contribution — reconstructing the earliest extant fiscal records — which adds new texture to that prior body of research.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer captures this relationship: D&V build on earlier work (like Brewer's) and add nuance through their reconstruction of fiscal records.

“Analysis of the earliest available records of a sovereign state's finances can be found not in the work of Brewer but in that of Drelichman and Voth” overstates the contrast with Brewer. The claim about “short-term problems with cash that forced Philip II to default on his debts” introduces specific causal claims the passage doesn't establish as the main point. The statement that Drelichman and Voth “assembled a novel collection of evidence” ignores the connection to prior scholarship that the passage explicitly draws.

The answer is D.
